台股與集中市場 ·

廣運自動化核心工程持續推進中 拿下台鐵公司1.89億元新標案 | 鉅亨網 - 台股新聞KY Lands NT$189 Million Taiwan Railways Contract as Automation Builds

https://news.cnyes.com/news/id/6461234

廣運（6125-TW）承接的桃園國際機場第三航廈行李處理系統今年將完工入帳；自動化核心工程亦同步推進，並以新台幣 1.89 億元標下國營台鐵新案。另，子公司盛新材料將發行 2 萬張現增，已報金管會，預估募資 7 億元。

廣運手頭重點工程包括桃機第三航廈行李處理系統、機捷 A14 車站行李處理設備、第二航廈行李暫存區暨北側登機廊行李輸送，以及國家圖書館物流中心與多起高科技製造案，構成營運成長支柱；其中第三航廈行李系統將於今年收尾並認列營收。

廣運 2026 年以「科技整合、落地應用、擴散價值」為策略主軸。公司表示，以 1.89 億元標得台鐵「傾斜式車廂 CCTV 與旅客服務資訊系統更新」，履約約 3 年；此一軌道相關訂單連同台北捷運自動電磁門、機捷 A14 行李系統等，皆屬軌道運輸業務鏈延伸。

廣運旗下的盛新材料（6930-TW）2025 年現增已成功募資 5 億元，今年再發行 2 萬張現增後，股本將由 5 億增至 7 億元；公司以每股暫訂 35 元發行 2 萬張，報件已递金管會。

持股結構：太極能源（4934-TW）約 42.28%、廣運 8.55%、鴻海（2317-TW）集團 7.10%。

盛新為國內少數可同時量產導電型（N-Type）與半絕緣型（SI-Type）碳化矽（SiC）基板業者：N-Type 切入 8 吋規格 SI-Type 維持 4、6 吋供貨覆蓋功率器件與高頻通信需求。

Kuang Yuan (6125-TW)'s baggage handling system for Taoyuan International Airport Terminal 3 will finish this year and be booked into revenue; core automation work is advancing in parallel, and the group also won a new state-owned Taiwan Railways (台鐵) contract for NT$189 million. Its subsidiary Sheng Xin New Materials (盛新材料) will issue 20,000 new shares via a capital increase—it has filed with the FSC—with an estimated raise of NT$700 million.

Kuang Yuan's flagship projects span Taoyuan Airport Terminal 3 baggage handling, Taoyuan Airport MRT line (機捷) A14 station baggage handling, Terminal 2 temporary baggage storage and conveyor work on the north boarding concourse, the National Library (國家圖書館) logistics center, and multiple high-tech manufacturing jobs—these underpin operating growth—with Terminal 3 baggage wrapping up this year for revenue recognition.

For 2026, Kuang Yuan anchors strategy on integrating technology, real-world deployment, and scaling value impact. It won Taiwan Railways’ ‘tilting-car CCTV and passenger information system upgrade’ tender for NT$189 million over roughly three years; together with Taipei Metro electromagnetic platform screen doors (自動電磁門), the Airport MRT A14 baggage system, this extends its rail-linked business chain.

Listed Sheng Xin New Materials (6930-TW) raised NT$500 million in its 2025 capital increase; this year’s issuance of another 20,000 new shares lifts share capital from NT$500 million to NT$700 million. The tentative issue price is NT$35 per share for 200,000 new shares—the filing has been submitted to the FSC.

Shareholding snapshot: Taiwan Solar Energy Materials Corp. / TSEC (4934-TW) about 42.28 percent, Kuang Yuan 8.55 percent, Hon Hai (Foxconn / 2317-TW) group 7.10 percent.

Sheng Xin is one of few domestic suppliers mass-producing both conductive-type (N-Type) and semi-insulating (SI-Type) silicon carbide (SiC) substrates: N-Type moves into 8-inch specs while SI-Type keeps 4- and 6-inch supply covering power devices and RF/high-frequency needs.
